description: OfficeIMO PDF conversion proof scenarios. The manifest is consumed by tests and the PDF visual review gallery so converter coverage stays observable.

compositionRoutes.diagnosticContract: Preserve OdfConversionResult diagnostics before PDF save; there is no direct OpenDocument PDF adapter yet.
Preserve OdfConversionResult diagnostics before PDF save; there is no direct OpenDocument PDF adapter yet.
Preserve OdfConversionResult diagnostics before PDF save; there is no direct OpenDocument PDF adapter yet.
A premium route still needs deterministic body selection, inline-resource resolution, attachment policy, and combined source/PDF diagnostics.
A premium route still needs book-level chapter order, stylesheet/font/image resolution, navigation, pagination, and combined diagnostics.
A premium route should preserve vector page output and hyperlinks; raster-only composition is not advertised as a direct Visio PDF converter.

qualityContract.goal: Premium PDF support means generic, reusable OfficeIMO.Pdf capabilities with thin source adapters, visible diagnostics for every accepted degradation, and repeatable artifact proof for creation, conversion, reading, manipulation, and review workflows.
qualityContract.runtimeOwnership: OfficeIMO.Pdf and OfficeIMO.Drawing own shared PDF layout, typography, reading, manipulation, diagnostics, compliance groundwork, and proof primitives. Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Markdown, HTML, RTF, OneNote, AsciiDoc, LaTeX, and Reader packages remain thin adapters over those primitives; OneNote.Markdown, AsciiDoc.Markdown, and Latex.Markdown own their explicit semantic projections and loss diagnostics.
nonGoals: No silent visual-only redaction or silent content loss.
No formal PDF/A, PDF/UA, Factur-X, or ZUGFeRD conformance claim without external validator proof.
No browser-grade HTML/CSS renderer claim for OfficeIMO.Html.Pdf.
No TeX execution or TeX-layout parity claim for OfficeIMO.Latex.Pdf.
No full editable PDF-to-Office reconstruction claim beyond declared logical-table reconstruction scenarios.
qualityContract.premiumClaimRule: A converter can be marked externally-verified only when its declared reference policy has stable source artifacts, producer/version metadata, page geometry, visual comparison results, text/link/structure proof, and no unexpected conversion warnings.
qualityContract.exactScopeRule: The exact fidelity level applies only to the named capability semantics and invariants. It never means whole-document pixel equivalence; visual distance and known approximations remain governed by the declared reference evidence.

scenarioQuality.owner: OfficeIMO.Html.Pdf over the OfficeIMO.Html shared render scene and OfficeIMO.Pdf
OfficeIMO.Html.Pdf over the shared OfficeIMO.Html and OfficeIMO.Drawing scene
OfficeIMO.Pdf redaction planner, applier, and verification
OfficeIMO.Pdf AcroForm filling, appearance generation, and flattening
OfficeIMO.Pdf typography primitives consumed by all PDF adapters
OfficeIMO.Pdf logical table analysis and OfficeIMO.Reader.Pdf
OfficeIMO.Pdf logical image model and OfficeIMO.Reader.Pdf
OfficeIMO.Pdf Standard security writer, reader, preflight, and page extraction
OfficeIMO.Pdf logical tables consumed by Word/Excel/PowerPoint PDF adapters

knownLimits: complex-script shaping beyond built-in scalar and Latin-ligature handling requires a caller-supplied IOfficeTextShapingProvider from OfficeIMO.Drawing

Office source fonts that are unavailable or cannot be embedded fall back to a mapped PDF font and retain an explicit conversion warning; available embeddable families are not limited to three compatibility slots
OneNote PDF is an explicit semantic-document projection and reports free-form canvas flattening and unresolved binary assets
formal PDF/A, PDF/UA, Factur-X, and ZUGFeRD claims require the external validator workflow for the exact artifact

expectedSimplifications: complex unsupported Word content records converter warnings rather than blocking export
unsupported workbook constructs are represented through warnings or skipped from the visible export
dashboard proof validates first-party worksheet rendering rather than pixel-perfect Excel application output
remote images require caller-supplied resolver and produce warnings when unresolved
the direct renderer implements a deterministic static HTML and paged-CSS contract rather than browser scripting or interactive state
static rendering does not execute JavaScript or reproduce interactive browser state
unsupported local resources remain visible through alternative text and stable diagnostics
HTML conversion uses the native static render scene and reports blocked resources instead of executing browser scripting
round-trip proof validates OfficeIMO logical/review contracts rather than browser-grade or pixel-perfect HTML rendering
invoice semantics remain represented as document text and tables rather than a formal e-invoice compliance profile
AsciiDoc is projected through the typed Markdown model; constructs without an equivalent remain visible as configured source fallbacks with combined parser, projection, and PDF diagnostics

LaTeX is not executed; the bounded semantic profile is projected through Markdown, while citations, math transport, package behavior, and unsupported constructs remain explicit warnings or visible source fallbacks
RTF to PDF is a visual fixed-layout export, while PDF to RTF is semantic text import from the OfficeIMO.Pdf logical model rather than lossless visual reconstruction

OneNote PDF is an explicit semantic-document projection; free-form canvas placement is flattened into reading order and unresolved images, attachments, recordings, and ink become readable placeholders with conversion warnings

The preservation matrix executes reusable OfficeIMO.Pdf rewrite scenarios and exports row classifications for rewrite-safe targeted mutations, preservation-drift detection, and blocked full rewrites for forms, tagged content, active content, and signatures; deeper object-level equivalence remains part of future parser hardening.
Redaction verification checks configured text markers and raw literal markers when available; complex encoded representations still rely on extractable-text proof unless a raw literal marker exists.

Fully covered page-level and safe nested form-XObject image placements are removed from the relevant content streams and XObject resources; shared form aliases are cloned before nested image removal. Simple partial intersections over 8-bit DeviceGray/DeviceRGB streams are rewritten at the pixel level, including Decode arrays and matching indirect 8-bit DeviceGray soft masks, and reused safe image/form invocations are isolated by cloning the target resource and renaming only the matched content-stream invocation. Transformed placements, color-key or explicit masks, JPEG payloads, and other complex image streams are reported by the planner and blocked by default during apply unless callers explicitly opt into a visual-only overlay.

The proof covers text, multiline text, comb text, scalar choice, multi-select choice, checkbox, and radio widgets with generated or preserved appearances; signed, encrypted, active-content, and complex JavaScript-driven forms remain blocked by preflight.
OfficeIMO.Pdf owns the provider contract and PDF glyph writing; callers supply the actual shaping engine when full script shaping is required.
Provider-backed warning suppression applies only to text/font runs actually shaped by the configured provider; declined runs keep the simplified-shaping diagnostics.
Automatically planned embedded-font fallback runs follow the same provider-owned warning contract as directly selected embedded fonts.
unsupported slide shapes and overflowing text boxes record converter warnings
PowerPoint proof validates first-party slide geometry and transforms rather than full Office renderer parity
visual layout remains adapter-specific while the shared typography contract requires Type0 Identity-H embedded font output and extractable Unicode text
logical readback remains parser-supported for born-digital PDFs and does not OCR scanned pages

textMarkers: Image geometry and table confidence marker

expectedSimplifications: active PDF actions are surfaced only as passive diagnostics and action summaries, and are not executed or exposed as script payload text
Reader chunks expose safe structural counters and typed form metadata rather than attempting editable PDF reconstruction

hostile PDF actions from nested name trees, catalog additional actions, page actions, and annotation actions are surfaced as passive diagnostics and action summaries, not executed or rendered as executable payload text
Reader JSON and positioned review HTML expose stable counters, scope/type metadata, and chained catalog action paths while keeping JavaScript, Launch, SubmitForm, ImportData, and Movie payloads inert
hostile layout readback exposes text and geometry signals without promising perfect human reading order
skewed image placement is reported as non-axis-aligned geometry rather than normalized into editable Office layout
headerless table-like bands are exposed with fallback column names and confidence diagnostics
jittered column positions are preserved as best-effort logical table geometry rather than editable spreadsheet reconstruction
image-only pages are routed to an OCR candidate contract instead of being treated as extracted native text
OfficeIMO.Reader.Pdf exposes candidate geometry, linked image asset metadata, and stable diagnostics but does not run OCR inside the dependency-light core
OfficeIMO.Reader can merge external OCR text back into generic ocr-text blocks/chunks with reader.ocr trace metadata
XFA packets are detected and reported as metadata without claiming XFA rendering or filling
OfficeIMO.Pdf exposes XFA packet names, payload counts, and template/datasets flags through the logical model and inspector
OfficeIMO.Reader.Pdf carries XFA facts in stable reader metadata for downstream routing
Standard security password read/decrypt is supported for generated and simple compatible PDFs
encrypted rewrite and form mutation still fail closed until encrypted preservation is implemented
expectedWarnings: encrypted rewrite is blocked by preflight even when the read password is valid

expectedSimplifications: logical readback is parser-supported and does not OCR scanned pages
positioned review HTML is an inspection surface, not a full PDF renderer; active catalog, page, and annotation actions are summarized as inert diagnostics without exposing action payloads
AcroForm XFA packets are represented as inert notice metadata and summary counters; OfficeIMO.Html.Pdf does not render or fill XFA
Word import is semantic editable reconstruction over parser-supported logical PDF objects, not fixed-layout page recreation
Excel and PowerPoint imports remain table-focused until broader editable reconstruction contracts are added

complete image-file payloads with transparency-mask fidelity metadata plus supported ImageMask stencil streams, color-key masked simple and Indexed streams, Decode-aware soft-mask-capable simple DeviceGray/DeviceRGB/basic-converted DeviceCMYK streams, basic ICCBased N=1/3/4 streams, and Decode-aware soft-mask-capable Indexed palette PDF image streams are embedded as native Word images when their filters are supported; pass-through JPEG image payloads with unresolved PDF transparency masks are embedded with explicit diagnostics; complex unsupported image streams and form widgets are represented as editable placeholders and diagnostics
supported internal PDF destinations are reconstructed as Word bookmark hyperlinks; unresolved, remote, or cross-document navigation actions remain diagnostics
